description_long: This object is a compact, cylindrical hand grenade with a dark green, slightly weathered metal body featuring multiple rectangular cutouts around its circumference. The top section is fitted with a robust, metallic safety lever and a prominent pull ring, both showing signs of wear and oxidation. The body is divided into two main segments by a horizontal seam, with the upper segment slightly narrower than the lower. The surface is matte with visible scuff marks and chipped paint, suggesting frequent handling or exposure to harsh conditions. The safety lever is secured by the pull ring, which is attached to a pin mechanism likely used to arm the device. No visible labels or digital components are present, giving it a utilitarian, military-grade appearance.
description: A rugged, dark green cylindrical hand grenade with a metal safety lever and pull ring, featuring rectangular cutouts and a weathered finish.
description_view_0: The grenade is shown from a three-quarter angle, highlighting the safety lever, pull ring, and the detailed cutouts on the body.
description_view_1: A direct side view reveals the full length of the safety lever and the alignment of the cutouts, with the pull ring partially visible.
description_view_2: Another three-quarter view from the opposite side, showing the lever and ring from a different angle, with the body cutouts clearly visible.
description_view_3: A rear view focuses on the back of the safety lever and the symmetrical arrangement of the cutouts, with minimal detail on the pull ring.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The grenade is armed by pulling the ring to remove the safety pin, allowing the lever to release and trigger the internal fuse mechanism.
